name: React Native
scope: A person with BASIC proficiency level in React Native (1-2 years experience) should be able to build and maintain simple to moderately complex mobile applications using React Native with Expo managed workflow. They understand the core React Native component library (View, Text, Image, ScrollView, FlatList, TextInput, TouchableOpacity, Pressable, SafeAreaView) and know when to use each component appropriately. They can compose components using props and children, manage local component state with useState, handle side effects with useEffect (data fetching, subscriptions, cleanup), and implement basic event handling (onPress, onChangeText, onSubmitEditing). They are comfortable with React Native's flexbox-based layout system (flexDirection, justifyContent, alignItems, flex) and can create responsive layouts using Dimensions or useWindowDimensions. They can style components using StyleSheet.create() and understand platform-specific styling with Platform.OS and Platform.select. They can implement basic screen navigation using React Navigation (Stack Navigator for screen-to-screen flow, Tab Navigator for bottom tabs) including passing parameters between screens and configuring header options. They understand FlatList for rendering lists with keyExtractor, renderItem, and basic performance props like initialNumToRender. They can fetch data from REST APIs using fetch or axios, handle loading and error states, and display results in the UI. They can build and validate simple forms with TextInput components, manage form state, and provide user feedback. They understand the component lifecycle in functional components, know how to use conditional rendering, and can handle basic platform differences. They use Expo CLI for development (expo start), test on Expo Go app or emulators, and understand basic debugging with console logs and React Native Debugger. Limitations include limited experience with complex navigation patterns (deep linking, nested navigators), advanced state management (Context with reducers, Redux), native modules, animations beyond basic LayoutAnimation, and performance optimization for large datasets.
